description:
walk_spec_for_discovery receives its caller's FnTypes by value after a .cloned().unwrap() (lines 544, 609, 647, 718). FnTypes contains four HashMaps (vars, block_envs, fn_constants, reachable_blocks) — cloning it inside a loop over all specs×outer-iterations is expensive.

Root cause: specs (immutable) and pending/callsite_fn_consts (mutable) are both needed in the same scope, so the borrow checker forces a clone of FnTypes out of specs before the mutable borrows begin.

Fix: change walk_spec_for_discovery's caller_ft parameter from FnTypes to &FnTypes. The call site holds a shared borrow of specs (&HashMap lookup) and passes &mut pending and &mut callsite_fn_consts as separate parameters — these are disjoint from specs, so the borrow checker is satisfied.

Requires fz-afs.5 (S1) to have stabilised the function signature first.
title: remove FnTypes clone in discovery walk
